code_verification: receiver method exists on the receiver type
static methods are called statically and member methods are called on valid instances
assignment target is compatible with the method return type
argument count, argument type, and overload shape match the public API
enum owner and enum value both exist
property constants use the correct owner and value type
signal callback signatures match the public signal typedef
code blocks do not rely on inferred namespaces or overloads from naming alone
draft_requirements: Write enough practical detail for application developers to use the feature in a dali-ui app.
Ground natural-language behavior claims in the actual public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when the context pack is not enough.
Before writing examples, inspect the referenced public headers and relevant sample files when needed.
Prefer examples copied or adapted from existing samples when they match the feature; otherwise keep examples small and verify every API use.
After drafting, review the completed Markdown line by line against repository evidence and fix inaccurate explanations or code before returning.
repair_requirements: Repair only the problematic spans and the smallest surrounding context needed; do not rewrite the whole document.
For each listed issue, use the repository evidence and context pack to verify the corrected wording or code before returning.
If an issue reveals the same API misuse nearby, fix the nearby equivalent misuse too; do not make unrelated style rewrites.
After repair, re-check the edited lines against public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when needed.

rules: Use dali-ui symbols as the app-facing API surface.
Use dali-core and dali-adaptor symbols only as setup/background unless the usage pattern explicitly marks them preferred.
Prefer View-based examples over raw Actor object-model examples.

usage_patterns.intent: Create and configure the primary object using public dali-ui APIs.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Prefer typed constructors and setters when available.

usage_patterns.intent: React to user or resource events through public signals.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Use signal connection APIs only with their owning trait or class.

rendered_prompt:
You are reviewing an existing DALi dali-ui application developer guide draft.
This is Phase 2.3.5: source-grounded prose review.

Primary goal:
- Improve natural-language accuracy and guide flow using repository evidence.
- Preserve the document's useful structure and examples whenever possible.
- Do not perform a full rewrite.
- Keep the revised user-facing guide in en only.
- Remove Korean, Japanese, Chinese, or other non-English prose from the revised guide.

Feature target:
- Feature: Label (label)
- Category: views-components
- Guide profile: dali-ui-app
- Audience: application developers using dali-ui public APIs

Review rules:
- Review every natural-language paragraph line by line against public headers, implementation files, samples, and UTCs when needed.
- Accuracy is the first priority. Flow is the second priority.
- Edit the smallest unit needed: word, sentence, paragraph, or section-local addition/removal.
- If a word-level change is enough, change only the word.
- If a sentence-level change is enough, change only the sentence.
- If an entire paragraph is based on a wrong premise, revise, add, or remove that paragraph.
- Preserve code blocks when possible.
- If prose can be corrected to match an already accurate code block, prefer changing prose.
- If the code block itself makes the prose inaccurate or breaks the guide flow, rewrite only the smallest necessary part of that code block and verify it against public headers/source.
- Do not invent APIs.
- Do not add generic filler just to make the document longer.
- Do not change section structure unless prose_review.allow_section_restructure is true or the current prose is clearly misleading.
- Prefer localized edits when prose_review.localized_edits_only is true.
- Preserve code blocks especially when prose_review.preserve_code_blocks is true.
- Prefer Dali::Ui::View, typed setters, fluent chaining, and view-tree idioms for app-facing guidance.
- Treat dali-core and dali-adaptor APIs as reference/context unless the feature explicitly requires them.

# Label

`Dali::Ui::Label` is the dali-ui view for displaying non-editable text in an application UI.

## Table of Contents

- [Create a Label](#create-a-label)
- [Configure Typography](#configure-typography)
- [Align and Lay Out Text](#align-and-lay-out-text)
- [Scale Text for UI Density](#scale-text-for-ui-density)
- [Use Anchors in Markup Text](#use-anchors-in-markup-text)
- [Render Asynchronously](#render-asynchronously)
- [Use Label Properties](#use-label-properties)

## Create a Label

Create text with `Dali::Ui::Label::New`. In dali-ui application code, treat the label as a `Dali::Ui::View` in your view tree while keeping the concrete `Dali::Ui::Label` handle when you need text-specific APIs.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/public-api/label.h>

Dali::Ui::Label CreateTitleLabel()
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("Account settings")
    .SetFontFamily("SamsungOneUI")
    .SetFontSize(24.0f)
    .SetFontWeight(Dali::Ui::Text::FontWeight::BOLD);
}

Dali::Ui::View CreateHeaderView()
{
  Dali::Ui::Label title = CreateTitleLabel();
  return title;
}
```

`Dali::Ui::Label` is non-editable. Use it for titles, captions, status text, helper text, and other display-only strings.

## Configure Typography

Use the typed setters on `Dali::Ui::Label` for font selection and style. `SetFontFamily`, `SetFontSize`, `SetFontWeight`, `SetFontWidth`, and `SetFontSlant` all return `Dali::Ui::Label&`, so they work well in fluent setup code.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreatePriceLabel()
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("$42.00")
    .SetFontFamily("SamsungOneUI")
    .SetFontSize(28.0f)
    .SetFontWeight(Dali::Ui::Text::FontWeight::SEMI_BOLD)
    .SetFontWidth(Dali::Ui::Text::FontWidth::NORMAL)
    .SetFontSlant(Dali::Ui::Text::FontSlant::NORMAL);
}
```

For variable fonts, use `SetFontVariation` with a settings string, or pass a `Dali::Vector<Dali::Ui::Text::FontVariationAxis>` when your code already has parsed axis values.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateVariableFontLabel()
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("Variable font")
    .SetFontFamily("SamsungOneUI")
    .SetFontSize(22.0f)
    .SetFontVariation("wght=700,wdth=90");
}
```

Read back the applied text configuration with getters such as `GetFontFamily`, `GetFontSize`, `GetFontWeight`, `GetFontWidth`, `GetFontSlant`, and `GetFontVariation`.

```cpp
void InspectTypography(const Dali::Ui::Label& label)
{
  Dali::String family = label.GetFontFamily();
  float size = label.GetFontSize();
  Dali::Ui::Text::FontWeight weight = label.GetFontWeight();
  Dali::Ui::Text::FontWidth width = label.GetFontWidth();
  Dali::Ui::Text::FontSlant slant = label.GetFontSlant();
}
```

## Align and Lay Out Text

Use `SetHorizontalTextAlignment` and `SetVerticalTextAlignment` to place text inside the label view. `Dali::Ui::Text::Alignment::START`, `Dali::Ui::Text::Alignment::CENTER`, and `Dali::Ui::Text::Alignment::END` are used for both axes.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateCenteredMessage()
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("No results")
    .SetFontSize(18.0f)
    .SetHorizontalTextAlignment(Dali::Ui::Text::Alignment::CENTER)
    .SetVerticalTextAlignment(Dali::Ui::Text::Alignment::CENTER);
}
```

Use `SetLineWrapMode` when a label can occupy multiple visual lines in a constrained layout. Use `SetLineHeight` to control line spacing. The line-height value is interpreted by the current `Dali::Ui::Text::LineHeightMode`; with the default relative mode, the value is a multiplier of the effective font size.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateBodyLabel()
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("Long body text can wrap cleanly in a dali-ui view.")
    .SetFontFamily("SamsungOneUI")
    .SetFontSize(16.0f)
    .SetLineWrapMode(Dali::Ui::Text::LineWrapMode::WORD)
    .SetLineHeight(1.4f)
    .SetHorizontalTextAlignment(Dali::Ui::Text::Alignment::START);
}
```

For direction-sensitive text, `SetLayoutDirectionMode` controls how the text layout direction is resolved.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateLocaleAwareLabel()
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("Localized label")
    .SetFontSize(16.0f)
    .SetLayoutDirectionMode(Dali::Ui::Text::LayoutDirectionMode::LOCALE);
}
```

Use `GetLineCount` after layout information is available when you need the number of lines for the current label width. Use `GetAsyncLineCount` when the label is configured for asynchronous rendering and you want the latest asynchronous result.

```cpp
void UpdateLineDependentState(Dali::Ui::Label label)
{
  int currentLineCount = label.GetLineCount();
  int latestAsyncLineCount = label.GetAsyncLineCount();
}
```

## Scale Text for UI Density

`SetFontSizeScale` multiplies the current font size. `SetMinimumFontSizeScale` and `SetMaximumFontSizeScale` constrain the final adjusted scale. If system text scaling is enabled with `SetSystemFontSizeScaleEnabled`, the system scale is combined with the label scale before the min/max constraints are applied.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateScalableLabel()
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("Readable text")
    .SetFontSize(18.0f)
    .SetFontSizeScale(1.1f)
    .SetMinimumFontSizeScale(0.85f)
    .SetMaximumFontSizeScale(1.4f)
    .SetSystemFontSizeScaleEnabled(true);
}
```

Use `GetAdjustedFontSizeScale` to inspect the scale that will be used after constraints and system scaling are resolved.

```cpp
float GetEffectiveScale(Dali::Ui::Label label)
{
  float requestedScale = label.GetFontSizeScale();
  float minimumScale = label.GetMinimumFontSizeScale();
  float maximumScale = label.GetMaximumFontSizeScale();
  float adjustedScale = label.GetAdjustedFontSizeScale();

  return adjustedScale;
}
```

## Use Anchors in Markup Text

`Dali::Ui::Label` supports anchor colors through `SetAnchorColor` and `SetAnchorClickedColor`. Anchor handling is exposed by `AnchorClickedSignal`, whose callback receives the clicked `Dali::Ui::View` and the anchor `href`.

```cpp
class HelpController : public Dali::ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  Dali::Ui::Label CreateHelpLabel()
  {
    Dali::Ui::Label label = Dali::Ui::Label::New(
      "Read the <a href='privacy'>privacy policy</a>.")
      .SetFontSize(16.0f)
      .SetMarkupEnabled(true)
      .SetAnchorColor(Dali::Ui::UiColor(0x1A73E8))
      .SetAnchorClickedColor(Dali::Ui::UiColor(0x174EA6));

    label.AnchorClickedSignal().Connect(this, &HelpController::OnAnchorClicked);
    return label;
  }

private:
  void OnAnchorClicked(Dali::Ui::View view, const Dali::String& href)
  {
    Dali::Ui::Label label = Dali::Ui::Label::DownCast(view);
    Dali::String clickedHref = href;
  }
};
```

Use `GetAnchorColor` and `GetAnchorClickedColor` if your application needs to synchronize label styling with the current UI theme.

```cpp
void CopyAnchorColors(Dali::Ui::Label source, Dali::Ui::Label target)
{
  target
    .SetAnchorColor(source.GetAnchorColor())
    .SetAnchorClickedColor(source.GetAnchorClickedColor());
}
```

## Render Asynchronously

For labels that are expensive to render or appear in scrolling content, enable asynchronous text rendering with `SetAsyncRendering`. The render result is reported by the label's asynchronous signals.

```cpp
class TextRenderController : public Dali::ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  Dali::Ui::Label CreateAsyncLabel()
  {
    Dali::Ui::Label label = Dali::Ui::Label::New("Prepared off the main render path")
      .SetFontSize(16.0f)
      .SetLineHeight(1.3f)
      .SetAsyncRendering(true)
      .SetRenderScale(1.5f);

    label.AsyncRenderFinishedSignal().Connect(
      this,
      &TextRenderController::OnAsyncRenderFinished);

    return label;
  }

private:
  void OnAsyncRenderFinished(Dali::Ui::View view, float width, float height)
  {
    Dali::Ui::Label label = Dali::Ui::Label::DownCast(view);
    int lines = label.GetAsyncLineCount();
  }
};
```

Use `SetRenderScale` only with asynchronous rendering. It rasterizes text at a larger scale and then downscales the result, which can improve quality when the view is visually scaled. The label layout size is not changed by `SetRenderScale`.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateScaledRenderLabel()
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("High quality scaled text")
    .SetFontSize(20.0f)
    .SetAsyncRendering(true)
    .SetRenderScale(2.0f);
}
```

## Use Label Properties

`Dali::Ui::Label::Property` owns the property IDs for label-specific text state. Prefer typed setters such as `SetFontSize`, `SetFontFamily`, `SetLineHeight`, `SetFontSizeScale`, and `SetAsyncRendering` in application setup code. Use property IDs when integrating with APIs that require a property index.

Common label property IDs include:

| Property ID | Value configured |
| --- | --- |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::TEXT` | Display text |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::FONT_FAMILY` | Font family |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::FONT_SIZE` | Font size in pixels |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::FONT_SIZE_SCALE` | Label font scale |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::MINIMUM_FONT_SIZE_SCALE` | Minimum allowed font scale |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::MAXIMUM_FONT_SIZE_SCALE` | Maximum allowed font scale |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::SYSTEM_FONT_SIZE_SCALE_ENABLED` | Whether system font scaling is applied |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT` | Horizontal text alignment |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::VERTICAL_ALIGNMENT` | Vertical text alignment |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::LINE_WRAP_MODE` | Wrapping strategy |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::LINE_HEIGHT` | Line-height value |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::LINE_HEIGHT_MODE` | Line-height interpretation |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::LAYOUT_DIRECTION_MODE` | Text direction resolution mode |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::MARKUP_ENABLED` | Markup parsing |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::ANCHOR_COLOR` | Anchor text color |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::ANCHOR_CLICKED_COLOR` | Clicked anchor color |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::ASYNC_RENDERING` | Asynchronous rendering |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::RENDER_SCALE` | Async text render scale |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::CUTOUT_ENABLED` | Cutout text rendering |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::TEXT_BACKGROUND_COLOR` | Background rendered behind glyphs |
| `Dali::Ui::Label::Property::TEXT_COLOR` | Text color |

The equivalent typed setup is clearer and keeps receiver types explicit:

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Label CreateStatusLabel()
{
  return Dali::Ui::Label::New("Connected")
    .SetFontFamily("SamsungOneUI")
    .SetFontSize(14.0f)
    .SetFontWeight(Dali::Ui::Text::FontWeight::MEDIUM)
    .SetFontSizeScale(1.0f)
    .SetLineHeight(1.2f)
    .SetAsyncRendering(false);
}
```
